name: TRUGS-AGENT Web Hub
description: Curated web resource graph — 3 branches, 50+ resources, weighted edges converging on TRUGS-AGENT

web_landscape.description: Web resources organized by audience and relevance to TRUGS-AGENT

properties.name: TRUGS-AGENT Web Hub
properties.purpose: Three-branch curated graph of web resources converging on TRUGS-AGENT adoption

properties.name: TRUGS-AGENT Repository
properties.purpose: The product — one file teaches your LLM TRUG/L, AAA, Memory, EPIC, Folder indexing, and Trugging

properties.name: TRUGS Specification Repository
properties.purpose: Full TRUG/L + TRUGS specification — 190-word vocabulary, CORE rules, validator, the complete formal system

properties.name: TRUGS Paper (Zenodo DOI)
properties.purpose: Academic paper — TRUGS specification, TRUG/L compilation algorithm, graph-sentence equivalence proof

properties.name: Nate B Jones
properties.purpose: Tech creator with 500K+ audience — potential TRUGS-AGENT evangelist and adoption channel

properties.name: Nate B Jones — YouTube
properties.purpose: YouTube channel — AI/dev tooling content reaching 500K+ audience, video format for TRUGS-AGENT demos

properties.name: Structured Agent Instructions
properties.purpose: Branch 1 — the problem of prompt ambiguity and solutions for formalized LLM instructions
properties.audience: LLM developers frustrated with prompt drift

properties.name: Specifications: The Missing Link to Making LLM Systems an Engineering Discipline
properties.purpose: UC Berkeley paper arguing that precise specification of expected behavior is the missing foundation for reliable LLM systems — the exact problem TRUG/L solves
properties.authors: Ion Stoica, Matei Zaharia et al.

properties.name: The Prompt Report: A Systematic Survey of Prompting Techniques
properties.purpose: 76-page survey covering 58 text prompting techniques from 1,500+ papers — the landscape TRUG/L simplifies to 190 words
properties.authors: Schulhoff et al. (OpenAI, Microsoft, Google, Stanford)

properties.name: IFEval: Instruction-Following Evaluation for Large Language Models
properties.purpose: Google Research benchmark with 25 verifiable instruction types and ~500 test prompts measuring LLM compliance with precise constraints

properties.name: Evaluating Goal Drift in Language Model Agents
properties.purpose: Quantifies how LLM agents progressively deviate from original instructions during long-context interactions — the drift TRUG/L prevents

properties.name: Prompting Is Programming: A Query Language for Large Language Models
properties.purpose: ETH Zurich paper proposing prompting-as-programming with constraints and types — TRUG/L takes this further with a fixed vocabulary that compiles to graphs

properties.name: AGENTIF: Benchmarking Instruction Following of LLMs as Agents
properties.purpose: Tsinghua benchmark finding even GPT-4 achieves only ~80% on simple unambiguous instructions — motivates formalized instruction languages

properties.name: DSPy — Programming, Not Prompting, Language Models
properties.purpose: Stanford framework replacing brittle prompts with composable Python modules — structured generation without a fixed vocabulary

properties.name: Guidance — Token-Level LLM Output Control
properties.purpose: Controls LLM output via Python-embedded grammars and CFGs — structural constraints at inference layer, not post-hoc

properties.name: LMQL — Query Language for LLMs
properties.purpose: SQL-like declarative constraints for LLMs — typed output specifications and multi-variable templates

properties.name: TypeChat — Schema Engineering for LLMs
properties.purpose: Microsoft tool replacing prompt engineering with TypeScript schema engineering — validates responses against types

properties.name: Outlines — Structured Generation via FSMs
properties.purpose: Compiles JSON schemas and regex into finite state machines for guaranteed-valid LLM output during token generation

properties.name: Instructor — Pydantic-Based Structured LLM Output
properties.purpose: Pydantic validation for LLM responses with automatic retries — available in Python, TypeScript, Go, Ruby, Rust, Elixir

properties.name: Structured Prompting with JSON: The Engineering Path to Reliable LLMs
properties.purpose: Introduces the 'Ambiguity Tax' concept — operational cost of vague prompts, reports 99%+ schema adherence with structured JSON prompting

properties.name: Anthropic — Claude Prompting Best Practices
properties.purpose: Official Anthropic guide recommending XML-tagged structure, role-based system prompts, and schema-first design

properties.name: What 1,200 Production Deployments Reveal About LLMOps in 2025
properties.purpose: Production retrospective marking the shift from prompt engineering to context engineering — architecting structured information, not tweaking wording

properties.name: Graph-Native Development
properties.purpose: Branch 2 — using graphs to describe, index, and navigate codebases and knowledge
properties.audience: Engineers building with knowledge graphs and code graphs

properties.name: Knowledge Graphs: Opportunities and Challenges
properties.purpose: Comprehensive survey covering KG construction, embedding, reasoning, and applications — the academic foundation for graph-based systems

properties.name: On the Evolution of Knowledge Graphs: A Survey and Perspective
properties.purpose: Covers static, dynamic, temporal, and event knowledge graphs with extraction and reasoning techniques

properties.name: LLM-Empowered Knowledge Graph Construction: A Survey
properties.purpose: How LLMs reshape the KG construction pipeline from ontology engineering through knowledge fusion — TRUGS automates this for codebases

properties.name: Neo4j — Graph Database
properties.purpose: Leading open-source property graph database with ACID transactions and Cypher query language — the heavyweight; TRUGS is the lightweight JSON alternative

properties.name: NetworkX — Network Analysis in Python
properties.purpose: Python library for graph creation, manipulation, and analysis — computation engine, not a specification format

properties.name: Joern — Code Analysis via Code Property Graphs
properties.purpose: Code analysis platform for C/C++/Java/Python/JS based on code property graphs — analyzes code as graphs, TRUGS indexes projects as graphs

properties.name: tree-sitter-graph — DSL for Graph Construction from Source Code
properties.purpose: Rust DSL for constructing arbitrary graph structures from parsed source code — AST to graph, bottom-up

properties.name: Emerge — Interactive Codebase Visualization
properties.purpose: Browser-based dependency visualization with graph metrics — visual where TRUGS is structural

properties.name: Code Property Graph — Specification and Query Language
properties.purpose: Formal spec of the code property graph data structure — security-focused code graph, TRUGS is project-focused

properties.name: Codebase Knowledge Graph: Code Analysis with Graphs
properties.purpose: Neo4j blog demonstrating Cypher queries for structural code analysis — heavy tooling, TRUGS does it with one JSON file

properties.name: Safely Restructure Your Codebase with Dependency Graphs
properties.purpose: Practical guide to using dependency graphs for safe refactoring of legacy codebases

properties.name: Software Dependency Graphs: Definition, Use Cases, and Implementation
properties.purpose: Overview of how dependency graphs represent file/class/function/library relationships in codebases

properties.name: GraphGen4Code — Code Knowledge Graph Toolkit
properties.purpose: IBM toolkit for building code knowledge graphs powering program search, code understanding, and bug detection

properties.name: JSON-LD 1.1 — W3C Recommendation
properties.purpose: W3C standard for encoding linked/graph data in JSON — bridges JSON simplicity with RDF expressiveness. TRUGS is simpler: no @context, no namespaces

properties.name: GQL — Graph Query Language (ISO/IEC 39075:2024)
properties.purpose: First new ISO database query language in 35+ years — complete DML/DDL for property graphs. TRUGS is a format, not a query language

properties.name: LLM Agent Protocols
properties.purpose: Branch 3 — development workflows, memory systems, and reliability patterns for LLM-powered agents

properties.name: Understanding the Planning of LLM Agents: A Survey
properties.purpose: Survey of planning in LLM agents — task decomposition, multi-step reasoning, replanning. AAA is a fixed 9-phase plan that removes the need for ad-hoc planning

properties.name: Memory for Autonomous LLM Agents: Mechanisms, Evaluation, and Frontiers
properties.purpose: Survey of agent memory architectures — episodic, semantic, procedural. TRUGS Memory is simpler: 4 types, markdown files, MEMORY.md index

properties.name: Agentic Reasoning for Large Language Models
properties.purpose: Unified roadmap bridging thought and action in LLM agents — personalization, long-horizon interaction, multi-agent scaling

properties.name: From LLM Reasoning to Autonomous AI Agents: A Comprehensive Review
properties.purpose: Reviews 2023-2025 agent frameworks integrating LLMs with modular toolkits for autonomous decision-making

properties.name: Building and Evaluating Alignment Auditing Agents
properties.purpose: Anthropic research on automated agent auditing — the problem AAA Phase 8 solves with spec-defined audit criteria

properties.name: LangGraph — Build Resilient Language Agents as Graphs
properties.purpose: Stateful multi-step agent workflows as directed graphs with persistence — graph-based execution, not graph-based specification

properties.name: CrewAI — Orchestrating Role-Playing Autonomous AI Agents
properties.purpose: Multi-agent framework with role-based crews and structured flows — orchestration without formalized instructions

properties.name: Microsoft AutoGen — Agentic AI Framework
properties.purpose: Multi-agent conversation framework enabling LLM/tool/human integration — agent chat without development protocol

properties.name: OpenHands — AI-Driven Software Development Platform
properties.purpose: Open-source coding agent with composable SDK and GitHub issue resolver — execution without plan-before-code protocol

properties.name: Claude Code Action — GitHub Actions Integration
properties.purpose: Official Anthropic action for Claude Code in CI/CD — autonomous agent in pipelines, uses CLAUDE.md for instructions

properties.name: Building Effective Agents
properties.purpose: Anthropic's foundational guide — human-in-the-loop checkpoints, stopping conditions, agents as LLMs in loops with tools. AAA formalizes this into 9 phases.

properties.name: Building Agents with the Claude Agent SDK
properties.purpose: Engineering guide on self-evaluating agents that check and improve their own output — three approaches to agent reliability

properties.name: 2025: The Year in LLMs — Simon Willison
properties.purpose: Year-end review covering rise of coding agents (Claude Code, Copilot CLI, OpenHands), IDE integration, autonomous development workflows

properties.name: Promptfoo — CI/CD for LLM Evaluation
properties.purpose: LLM evaluation in CI/CD pipelines with quality gates and automated red-teaming — testing without formalized specs

properties.name: Awesome Memory for Agents — Tsinghua C3I
properties.purpose: Curated paper collection on agent memory — episodic, semantic, procedural memory mechanisms and persistence strategies

properties.path: Prompt ambiguity → TRUG/L solves it → TRUGS-AGENT delivers it
Graph tooling → FOLDER indexes it → TRUGS-AGENT delivers it
Agent reliability → AAA/Memory/EPIC solve it → TRUGS-AGENT delivers it

properties.connection: Paper argues specifications are missing — TRUG/L is that specification
Drift is the problem — fixed vocabulary prevents it
Instruction following benchmarks — TRUG/L makes instructions unambiguous
80% compliance on simple instructions — TRUG/L pushes toward 100%
58 prompting techniques — TRUG/L replaces all of them with one language
Prompting as programming — TRUG/L takes it further with graph compilation

properties.difference: DSPy optimizes prompts automatically — TRUG/L eliminates ambiguity by design
Guidance constrains output tokens — TRUG/L constrains input instructions
TypeChat validates output against schema — TRUG/L validates instructions against vocabulary
Outlines ensures valid output format — TRUG/L ensures valid input specification
Instructor validates responses — TRUG/L validates instructions

properties.connection: Recommends structured prompts — TRUG/L is the most structured prompt
Shift from prompt engineering to context engineering — TRUG/L is the context engineering language
Ambiguity Tax concept — TRUG/L eliminates the tax

properties.difference: Neo4j is a database — TRUGS is a specification format. Neo4j requires a server; TRUGS is one JSON file
Joern analyzes code as graphs — TRUGS indexes projects as graphs. Different granularity
tree-sitter-graph builds graphs from ASTs — TRUGS builds graphs from project structure
Emerge visualizes — TRUGS specifies. Different output: visual vs machine-readable
Graph4Code builds code KGs for search — TRUGS builds project KGs for navigation
JSON-LD links to the semantic web — TRUGS links to nothing external. Simpler: no @context, no namespaces
GQL queries graphs — TRUG/L describes graphs as sentences. Query language vs specification language

properties.connection: LLMs building KGs — TRUGS is the format they build into
Shows codebase-as-graph value — TRUGS does it without Neo4j
Dependency graphs for refactoring — folder.trug.json is the persistent index

properties.relationship: NetworkX can load and analyze TRUGS graphs

properties.connection: Anthropic's agent patterns — AAA formalizes them into 9 mandatory phases
Agent auditing research — AAA Phase 3 defines criteria, Phase 8 executes audit
Ad-hoc planning survey — AAA replaces ad-hoc with fixed 9-phase protocol
Complex memory architectures — TRUGS Memory is 4 types in markdown files
Self-evaluating agents — AAA audit cycle is the formalized version

properties.difference: LangGraph executes workflows as graphs — TRUGS specifies projects as graphs. Runtime vs specification
CrewAI orchestrates multiple agents — TRUGS-AGENT teaches one agent to work correctly
AutoGen enables agent chat — TRUGS-AGENT enables agent discipline
OpenHands is a coding platform — TRUGS-AGENT is a methodology that works in any platform

properties.relationship: Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md — TRUGS-AGENT IS the CLAUDE.md

properties.connection: Landscape overview — shows the ecosystem TRUGS-AGENT operates in

properties.relationship: Promptfoo tests LLM output — TRUG/L makes the input testable too

properties.connection: Reviews 2023-2025 agent frameworks — TRUGS-AGENT is the 2026 answer
